title: Promotion
description:
Promotion entity that can be used for dynamic promotions, where the business unit is given
from the Basket, where it is contained.

allOf.description: Common properties for both local and group promotions.

id.description: The unique identifier of the promotion.

revision.description:
The revision of the entity. Must be updated when there is a change to the entity. The
new revision must have a larger numeric value than the previous revision of the entity.
If the revision number is smaller than the previous one, then the input will be
considered as outdated and will be ignored since there is already a newer version of it
in our system.

The revision property is used to validate the version of an entity. In Hii Retail we
will automatically use an EPOCH timestamp with millisecond precision if not provided.
We also strongly recommend external systems to provide the revision as an EPOCH numeric
value with millisecond precision. This approach ensures precise versioning and
simplifies comparison, validation and sorting of entity versions.

reportingId.description:
If provided, the reportingId will get added to the POSLog to support aggregation of separate promotions.
If you for example have a recurring campaign where the offers differ from time to time, this allows you to follow up
the performance of the campaign rather than the individual promotions and offers.

conflictResolutionType.description:
Determines how this promotion competes with other promotions.
The enum is configurable per tenant. Each type has a configurable relationship to all other types
where the individual relationship defines how the system shall resolve a conflict between the two types.
Note that promotions with the same conflictResolutionType will always apply BESTDEAL as resolution strategy if they would conflict.

status.description: The current status of the promotion.

name.description: Default text shown in frontend applications.

receiptText.description:
Text shown on printed receipt as a label to the rewarded discount.
If not set, name will be used.

validFrom.description:
The start date and time for the promotion.
If the time is left out, 00:00:00.000 is assumed

validTo.description:
The end date and time for the promotion.
If the time is left out, 23:59:59.999 is assumed.

schedule.description:
Controls when the promotion is active within the validFrom/validTo window using a
cron-like expression. All evaluation happens in UTC; pre-shift the expression if you
need a store-local window.

Format (5 space-separated fields): "minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week"
- Field ranges:
  - minute: 0-59
  - hour: 0-23 (24-hour clock)
  - day-of-month: 1-31
  - month: 1-12 (January = 1)
  - day-of-week: 0-7 (cron convention, Saturday = 6; both 0 and 7 mean Sunday)
- Supported tokens (per field):
  - "*": every value in the field's range
  - "N": a single value
  - "N-M": inclusive range, requires N <= M (example: "9-14" means hours 09:00:00
    through 14:59:59, exclusive of 15:00)
  - "N,M,...": comma-separated list of values or ranges
  - "*/S": every Sth value across the full range, S >= 1 (example: "*/5" in the
    minute field means every five minutes)
  - "N-M/S": every Sth value across the range [N, M]
- The minute field IS evaluated. Use "*" for "every minute"; "0" restricts to the
  top of the hour only.
- Named tokens are accepted in two fields only: day-of-week
  (SUN, MON, TUE, WED, THU, FRI, SAT) and month (JAN, FEB, ... DEC). Names are
  three-letter and case-insensitive ("Mon" == "MON" == "mon"). Day-of-week names
  follow the same cron convention as the numbers (SUN = 0). Within a single range
  the two endpoints must be both-named ("MON-FRI") or both-numeric ("1-5") — they
  may not be mixed ("MON-5" is rejected). The step value after "/" is always
  numeric, so a named range may still be stepped ("MON-FRI/2" is valid).
  minute, hour, and day-of-month accept numbers only.
- Day-of-week and month ranges wrap around: in these two fields a descending range
  crosses the field's top back to its bottom instead of being rejected. "FRI-TUE"
  ("5-2") means Fri, Sat, Sun, Mon, Tue; "FRI-SUN" ("5-0") means Fri, Sat, Sun;
  "DEC-APR" ("12-4") means Dec, Jan, Feb, Mar, Apr; "JUN-JAN" ("6-1") means Jun through
  Dec plus Jan. A non-descending range is unchanged, so "SUN-SUN" ("0-0") and "DEC-DEC"
  ("12-12") stay a single value. Wrap-around applies to the day-of-week and month
  fields only; descending minute, hour, and day-of-month ranges are rejected.
- Aliases such as "@daily"/"@weekly", full names ("MONDAY"), and the unix-cron
  extensions "L", "W", "#", "?" are NOT supported.

Evaluation rules:
- If a field is "*", the entire span of that field is considered (that is,
  "* * 1 1 *" means the whole day on Jan 1).
- When BOTH day-of-month and day-of-week are restricted (neither is "*"), they are
  combined with AND: the promotion fires only on dates matching both. (This differs
  from classical unix cron, which ORs the two fields.)

Timezone / DST (worked example): there is no timezone awareness, so a store-local
window must be pre-shifted to UTC by the store's current offset AND re-edited when
that offset changes at a daylight-saving boundary. A Europe/Stockholm store wanting
"15:00-15:59 local" writes "* 13 * * *" in summer (CEST = UTC+2; 15:00 local =
13:00 UTC) but "* 14 * * *" in winter (CET = UTC+1; 15:00 local = 14:00 UTC). The
summer expression "* 13 * * *" matches 2026-06-04T13:30:00Z (15:30 local) but not
2026-06-04T14:00:00Z (16:00 local), and would fire an hour early in local terms once
the store falls back to UTC+1.

upsellRules.description:
List of upsell information; relevant only if upsell properties
are set on conditions.

id.description: The id that is referenced from conditions

thresholdAmount.description:
The amount that triggers the upsell message. Example, if you have a promotion "Buy for 500 get 20% off",
you can set thresholdAmount to 300 to show an upsell message when the customer has gone over 300 but not before.

thresholdQuantity.description:
The quantity that triggers the upsell message. Example, if you have a promotion "Buy 3 for 2",
you can set thresholdQuantity to 1 or 2 to show an upsell message when the customer has bought 1 or 2.

channels.description:
Restrict to target sales channels so that a particular message is only shown in Scan&Go for instance.
No restriction if left out.

upsellDisplayMessage.description: Optional upsell message to be shown to the user.
upsellDisplayMessage.example: Buy another to unlock extra savings!

upsellImageUrl.description: Optional image URL to show an image in the upsell message.

additionalProperties.description:
Arbitrary tenant-defined key-value pairs attached to this promotion.
These values are not evaluated as promotion conditions.
They are passed through unchanged onto every Discount produced when
this promotion fires, enabling POSLog enrichment.

activators.description:
Defines conditions that must be met to activate the promotion.
If multiple properties are set, they are combined with a logical AND.

discountCode.description:
Specific discount code that must be provided by the customer.
When used, the promotion will not be sent to third party systems, like shelf labels.

coupon.description:
Physical or digital coupon code requirement.
When used, the promotion will not be sent to third party systems, like shelf labels.

id.description: The coupon ID or coupon code.

providerId.description:
If multiple providers are used for coupons, this optional providerId is used to uniquely
distinguish between coupon IDs and to denote which provider to redeem the coupon against.
Information about the providerId is included in the POSLog.

b2b.description:
Business to business qualification. At least one of the properties must be provided.
When multiple properties are set, all of them must match (AND logic).
When used, the promotion will not be sent to third party systems, like shelf labels.

agreementId.description:
Allows this specific promotion to serve as a price agreement where a B2B customer may have
several price agreements. For a B2B customer to get access to a specific agreement it must be
part of the customer's profile.

projectId.description:
A specific B2B project identifier. Can be combined with any other B2B property;
when multiple properties are set, the basket must match all of them (AND logic).

isB2B.description: A general condition that applies to all B2B customers

loyalty.description:
Loyalty qualification.
When used, the promotion will not be sent to third party systems, like shelf labels.
Either the segment or the active flag should be set.

active.description: If true, the promotion is only available to loyalty customers.

segment.description:
A customer segment that is defined on the loyalty system. Some examples are geographical,
demographical and spend level segments.

staff.description:
Staff member qualification.
When used, the promotion will not be sent to third party systems, like shelf labels.

active.description: If true, the promotion is only available to staff members.

tender.description:
Future-use tender-based qualification.
When used, the promotion will not be sent to third party systems, like shelf labels.

channels.description:
Restricts the promotion to specific sales channels or application types within the businessUnit.
If empty or left out, the promotion applies to all channels.

offers.description:
A list of offers that can be applied to this promotion. Discounts can be constructed
using either this 'offers' structure or the simpler 'campaignPrices' structure.
Note that offers within a promotion that conflict with each other
(e.g. offer A: 20% off on shirts, offer B: 30% off on shirts) will apply BESTDEAL as resolution.
I.e. the offer with the highest monetary value for the customer will be applied.

oneOf.description: Container for a specific set of conditions and resulting reward within a promotion.

id.description:
The id of the offer. The id shall be unique within the promotion but does not necessarily
have to be globally unique. The id will be reported back in the POSLog.

conditions.description: Conditions required to qualify for this offer.

items.description:
A single condition that must be met for the associated reward to be applied.
Multiple conditions within an offer will have an AND relationship.

upsellRule.description: Reference to a rule that is defined on Promotion level

itemSet.description:
Conditions the offer to a defined set of items with an optional minimum quantity or amount. This allows the creation
of both simple offers like "20% off on all items in category 'shirt'" as well as more complex constructs like "buy 4 chairs
(itemSet with a minimum quantity 4) and 1 table (separate itemSet with minimum quantity 1) and get the table at half price".
A condition without `filters` set will automatically match all items.

itemSetId.description: Identifier for the item set. Can be used as target from a reward.

filters.description:
Include or exclude items based on item properties. At least one filter property
must be provided.

filterMode.description:
Whether to include or exclude items matching the filters. This allows you to INCLUDE itemCategory 'shoes'
and EXCLUDE brand 'Adidas' as an example.

itemCategories.description: List of item categories to filter by

itemIds.description: List of specific item IDs to filter by

gtinPrefixes.description:
List of GTIN prefix values. These prefixes are only used for promotions provided in
the Basket (dynamic promotions). They are ignored for promotions sent via the Promotion
Input API.

modelIds.description: List of model IDs to filter by. Represented by a Model item link in the Item Link API.

brands.description: List of brands to filter by

colors.description: List of colors to filter by

sizes.description: List of sizes to filter by

seasons.description: List of seasons to filter by

gs1ApplicationIdentifiers.description:
GS1 application identifier based restrictions.
If set, we do not send this to third party integrations, like shelf labels.

bestBeforeDateDaysRemaining.description: BEST BEFORE DATE (less than days left).

upsellRule.description: Reference to an upsell rule that is defined on Promotion level

minQuantity.description:
In general only relevant for unit type items but can be used for example in 'buy at least
3 kg of ...' type offers.
If greater than 1, we do not send this to third party integrations, like shelf labels.

minAmount.description:
Minimum monetary spend required to fulfill itemSet.
If set, we do not send this to third party integrations, like shelf labels.

reward.description: Reward granted when this offer's conditions are met.

maxRepeat.description:
Defines allowed number of repetitions the conditions can be fulfilled and result in a reward.
This allows for limitations such as "only 2 per customer".
No limit if not set.

contributorDistribution.description:
Controls how the reward value is distributed across contributing items.
Only meaningful on unit rewards, which scope the discount to a reward
target item set. Total rewards always distribute across all
condition-matched items and therefore do not accept this field.
  * `ONLY_REWARD` (default): distributed only across the items in the
    reward's target item set. Matches legacy behaviour.
  * `ALL_CONTRIBUTORS`: distributed proportionately across all items
    contributing to the offer's conditions.

amount.description: The new discounted price to set as the unit price of the targeted items.

quantityLimit.description: How many of the eligible items in the targeted itemSets that shall be discounted.

amount.description:
The new total price of the package. A package is all of the targeted items. The amount
will be distributed proportionally over those items.

targetItemSets.description:
Reference to one or more itemSetId. Targeting more than one item set from
the reward is useful for distributed discounts.

amount.description:
The amount to reduce the unit price, if the quantityLimit is 1, or the package price
if quantityLimit is greater than 1.

quantityLimit.description: How many of the eligible items in the targeted itemSets that shall be discounted.

targetItemSets.description:
Reference to one or more itemSetId. Targeting more than one item set from
the reward is useful for distributed discounts.

percent.description: The percent to reduce the item by.

amountLimit.description: Optional cap on total reward amount.

quantityLimit.description: How many of the eligible items in the targeted itemSets that shall be discounted.

targetItemSets.description:
Reference to one or more itemSetId. Targeting more than one item set from
the reward is useful for distributed discounts.

percent.description:
The percentage value that should be applied on top of the purchase price to give the
discounted sales price.

value.description: Populates the transaction with information to be consumed by other applications.

items.description:
Represents a single level within a tiered reward structure.
Each tier combines a mandatory qualification threshold — either a minimum item quantity (thresholdQuantity)
or a monetary spend (thresholdAmount) — with a specific resulting reward.

If the reward (TotalReward or UnitReward) contains a quantityLimit, it should only be (optionally)
set on the last tier to provide a total cap on the tiers.

Tiered Total Offers:
For total-based rewards, the threshold is typically based on total spend (thresholdAmount)
to trigger a total discount.
Example: Buy for 100€ and get 10€ total discount.

Tiered Item Offers:
For item-based rewards, the tier must be associated with an item set (via targetItemSets in the reward).
The threshold can be either quantity-based (thresholdQuantity) or amount-based (thresholdAmount).
Example: Buy more than 5 from itemSet X and get 10% discount.

Recursive declarations of tiered rewards are not allowed.

thresholdAmount.description: Minimum monetary spend required to qualify for this tier.

thresholdQuantity.description: Minimum quantity required to qualify for this tier.

maxRepeat.description:
Defines allowed number of repetitions the conditions can be fulfilled and result in a reward.
This allows for limitations such as "only 2 per customer".
No limit if not set.

amount.description: The new discounted price to set as the unit price of the targeted items.

quantityLimit.description: How many of the eligible items in the targeted itemSets that shall be discounted.

amount.description:
The new total price of the package. A package is all of the targeted items. The amount
will be distributed proportionally over those items.

targetItemSets.description:
Reference to one or more itemSetId. Targeting more than one item set from
the reward is useful for distributed discounts.

amount.description:
The amount to reduce the unit price, if the quantityLimit is 1, or the package price
if quantityLimit is greater than 1.

quantityLimit.description: How many of the eligible items in the targeted itemSets that shall be discounted.

targetItemSets.description:
Reference to one or more itemSetId. Targeting more than one item set from
the reward is useful for distributed discounts.

percent.description: The percent to reduce the item by.

amountLimit.description: Optional cap on total reward amount.

quantityLimit.description: How many of the eligible items in the targeted itemSets that shall be discounted.

targetItemSets.description:
Reference to one or more itemSetId. Targeting more than one item set from
the reward is useful for distributed discounts.

percent.description:
The percentage value that should be applied on top of the purchase price to give the
discounted sales price.

campaignPrices.description:
This represents a campaign for one or more items and is used instead of offers to add
simple "new price for item" campaigns. Note that BESTDEAL will be applied as resolution strategy if the
same item occurs multiple times as a campaign price or if it conflicts with one or several offers in the promotion.

unitPrice.description: The new price of this item
